frame_analyses.description: Title card: 'Intracorporeal Lap Suturing & Lap Tumble Square Knot - Advanced Techniques for Sx Trainees | Suturing Series, Part 8.' Instructional overlay present.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content framing — not a trainee performance

frame_analyses.description: Wide view of FLS trainer box with Penrose drain suspended horizontally between two clamps. Two needle drivers visible (black-handled and silver-handled). Suture and drain clearly positioned.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Equipment setup shown. Training context confirmed.

frame_analyses.description: Silver needle driver grasping needle; black driver positioned to receive. Suture entry point appears to approach marked line on drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstration of needle load and initial approach. Camera angle shows overhead perspective.

frame_analyses.description: Needle driven through first mark. Entry appears perpendicular to drain surface. Suture loop forming above drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean entry technique demonstrated. Perpendicular approach avoids shallow bites.

frame_analyses.description: Suture fully passed through both marks. Drain slit clearly visible. Suture emerges cleanly on far side.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both entry and exit points within visible range. No apparent deviation from marks.

frame_analyses.description: Black needle driver (left hand) wrapping suture around silver driver (right hand). Double wrap forming for surgeon's knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right hand leading first throw. Clear double-wrap formation visible.

frame_analyses.description: First throw being cinched with controlled tension. Knot forming at drain center. Suture tails visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth tensioning without jerking. Knot appears secure.

frame_analyses.description: Silver driver (right hand) now leading wrap around black driver (left hand). Hand switch from first throw confirmed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Correct hand alternation. Left hand now dominant in throw sequence.

frame_analyses.description: Single throw cinched. Knot appears tight against drain. Both suture tails still visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean single throw with appropriate tension.

frame_analyses.description: Black driver (left hand) wrapping around silver driver (right hand). Third throw initiated.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch confirmed again. Right hand now leading.

frame_analyses.description: Final throw cinched. Knot fully formed at drain midline. Suture tails positioned for cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: All three throws completed with consistent hand alternation. Knot appears secure and centered.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic scissors positioned at suture tail. First tail being cut.
Both suture tails cut. Knot fully seated at drain. No visible gap in slit.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion demonstrated. Drain slit appears closed around knot.

confidence_rationale: This is instructional/educational content, not a trainee performance. Video contains narrated title cards, setup demonstrations, and teaching overlays. No actual timed performance is present. Per classification rules, instructional content is NOT scored — teaching content is extracted instead.
cannot_determine: Completion time — no stopwatch or performance clock visible
Actual trainee/surgeon performance — this is a structured tutorial demonstration
FLS score — instructional content does not receive FLS scores
technique_summary: This video is an expert-level tutorial demonstrating proper intracorporeal suturing and square knot technique. The demonstration shows correct needle placement, perpendicular entry/exit, proper hand alternation across three throws (double-throw surgeon's knot followed by two single throws), and clean suture cutting. The Penrose drain setup, mark-to-mark suture placement, and final knot positioning all exemplify expert technique.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper needle driver grasp at 2/3 point from needle tip
Perpendicular needle entry to avoid shallow bites
Controlled suture passage through both marks
Surgeon's knot formation (double throw) with right-hand lead
Single-throw alternation between dominant and non-dominant hand
Consistent hand switching across all three throws
Knot tensioning with smooth, controlled motion
Proper scissors approach for suture tail cutting
Final knot positioning at drain midline
verbal_cues: Title: 'Intracorporeal Lap Suturing & Lap Tumble Square Knot'
Subtitle: 'Advanced Techniques for Sx Trainees | Suturing Series, Part 8'
Closing: "Remember, surgery trainees, that in the operating room and in life, practice is the master key that unlocks the door to success."
Closing: "Keep refining those skills, and until next time, happy suturing!"
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding shallow/tangential needle entry by maintaining perpendicular approach
Preventing knot slippage through controlled tension application
Avoiding gaps in drain slit closure by proper knot positioning
Ensuring hand alternation across all three throws (not repeating hand sequences)

improvement_suggestions: This is tutorial content demonstrating expert technique — no performance deficiencies identified
